clean_story: Heat moving.  Work shifting hands. "Energy transfer" names the silent trade—how warmth, motion, or power jumps from one place to another, never created or destroyed, only passed like a baton. Picture a campfire:—Flames don’t give you heat. They shake their own molecules fast, which bump air molecules, which jostle your skin’s molecules—each collision a tiny hand-off of energy. Same with a swinging pendulum:At the top, it holds stillness as "potential. "Dropping, that stillness becomes speed—kinetic energy—until friction steals it, warming the air instead. Even a battery is just stored chemical energy waiting to rush into wires as electrons, lighting a bulb or spinning a motor. No energy vanishes. A car’s "lost" fuel becomes exhaust heat, road friction, sound. Three related subjects are entropy growth, work efficiency and thermal bridges.
